turns.text: For a 3-day hike in cold, wet weather I'd recommend the Stormridge 3L Insulated Rain Jacket. It's a waterproof 3-layer shell with synthetic insulation rated from 5°C down to -10°C, with sealed seams and pit zips for venting on the climb. It's $189 and we have 42 in stock.

citations.title: Stormridge 3L Insulated Rain Jacket — product page
Contoso Outdoor — Cold & wet weather buying guide

turns.text: For sub-zero temperatures the Polaris -15 Sleeping Bag is the right choice. It's rated to -15°C comfort / -22°C limit, with 850-fill-power hydrophobic down so it keeps performing if it gets damp. $419, currently 8 left in stock.

Two strong options under 2 kg: the Skylight 2 Ultralight Tent (1.78 kg packed, $369) is the lightest and has two doors and two vestibules. The Basecamp 2 Trekking Tent (2.4 kg, $279) is heavier but roomier and cheaper. If grams matter, Skylight 2; if headroom matters, Basecamp 2.

Contoso Outdoor accepts returns within 60 days of purchase for any unused item with original tags. Boots that have been worn outdoors are not returnable, but unworn boots tried on indoors are fine. Start a return from the order page or in the chat at any time.

For wet trails I'd point you at the Riverstone Waterproof Hiking Shoe (Women's). It's a low-cut shoe with a Gore-Tex membrane and a Vibram sole that grips well on slick rock. $159, with 31 pairs in stock.

citations.title: Riverstone Waterproof Hiking Shoe — product page

fallback.text: I don't have a confident answer for that in the Contoso catalog. Could you share more about the activity, temperature range, or budget? I can also hand you off to a human associate if you'd prefer.
